1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم قد أفلح المؤمنون
1.english: Certainly will the believers have succeeded:
1.transliteration: Qad aflahal mu'minoon

1.arabic: الذين هم في صلاتهم خاشعون
1.english: They who are during their prayer humbly submissive
1.transliteration: Allazeena hum fee Salaatihim khaashi'oon

1.arabic: والذين هم عن اللغو معرضون
1.english: And they who turn away from ill speech
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum 'anillaghwimu'ridoon

1.arabic: والذين هم للزكاة فاعلون
1.english: And they who are observant of zakah
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum liz Zakaati faa'iloon

1.arabic: والذين هم لفروجهم حافظون
1.english: And they who guard their private parts
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum lifuroo jihim haafizoon

1.arabic: إلا على أزواجهم أو ما ملكت أيمانهم فإنهم غير ملومين
1.english: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they will not be blamed -
1.transliteration: Illaa 'alaaa azwaajihim aw maa malakat aimaanuhum fa innahum ghairu maloomeen

1.arabic: فمن ابتغى وراء ذلك فأولئك هم العادون
1.english: But whoever seeks beyond that, then those are the transgressors -
1.transliteration: Famanib taghaa waraaa'a zaalika fa ulaaa'ika humul 'aadoon

1.arabic: والذين هم لأماناتهم وعهدهم راعون
1.english: And they who are to their trusts and their promises attentive
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum li amaanaatihim wa 'ahdihim raa'oon

1.arabic: والذين هم على صلواتهم يحافظون
1.english: And they who carefully maintain their prayers -
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum 'alaa Salawaatihim yuhaafizoon

1.arabic: أولئك هم الوارثون
1.english: Those are the inheritors
1.transliteration: Ulaaa'ika humul waarisoon

1.arabic: الذين يرثون الفردوس هم فيها خالدون
1.english: Who will inherit al-Firdaus. They will abide therein eternally.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yarisoonal Firdawsa hum feehaa khaalidoon

1.arabic: ولقد خلقنا الإنسان من سلالة من طين
1.english: And certainly did We create man from an extract of clay.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad khalaqnal insaana min sulaalatim minteen

1.arabic: ثم جعلناه نطفة في قرار مكين
1.english: Then We placed him as a sperm-drop in a firm lodging.
1.transliteration: Summa ja'alnaahu nutfatan fee qaraarim makeen

1.arabic: ثم خلقنا النطفة علقة فخلقنا العلقة مضغة فخلقنا المضغة عظاما فكسونا العظام لحما ثم أنشأناه خلقا آخر ۚ فتبارك الله أحسن الخالقين
1.english: Then We made the sperm-drop into a clinging clot, and We made the clot into a lump [of flesh], and We made [from] the lump, bones, and We covered the bones with flesh; then We developed him into another creation. So blessed is Allah, the best of creators.
1.transliteration: Summa khalaqnan nutfata 'alaqatan fakhalaqnal 'alaqata mudghatan fakhalaq nal mudghata 'izaaman fakasawnal 'izaama lahman summa anshaanaahu khalqan aakhar; fatabaarakal laahu ahsanul khaaliqeen

1.arabic: ثم إنكم بعد ذلك لميتون
1.english: Then indeed, after that you are to die.
1.transliteration: Summa innakum ba'da zaalika la maaiyitoon

1.arabic: ثم إنكم يوم القيامة تبعثون
1.english: Then indeed you, on the Day of Resurrection, will be resurrected.
1.transliteration: Summa innakum Yawmal Qiyaamati tub'asoon

1.arabic: ولقد خلقنا فوقكم سبع طرائق وما كنا عن الخلق غافلين
1.english: And We have created above you seven layered heavens, and never have We been of [Our] creation unaware.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad khalaqnaa fawqakum sab'a taraaa'iqa wa maa kunnaa 'anil khalqi ghaafileen

1.arabic: وأنزلنا من السماء ماء بقدر فأسكناه في الأرض ۖ وإنا على ذهاب به لقادرون
1.english: And We have sent down rain from the sky in a measured amount and settled it in the earth. And indeed, We are Able to take it away.
1.transliteration: Wa anzalnaa minas samaaa'i maaa'am biqadarin fa-askannaahu fil ardi wa innaa 'alaa zahaabim bihee laqaa diroon

1.arabic: فأنشأنا لكم به جنات من نخيل وأعناب لكم فيها فواكه كثيرة ومنها تأكلون
1.english: And We brought forth for you thereby gardens of palm trees and grapevines in which for you are abundant fruits and from which you eat.
1.transliteration: Fa anshaanaa lakum bihee Jannaatim min nakheelinw wa a'naab; lakum feehaa fawaakihu kaseeratunw wa minhaa taakuloon

1.arabic: وشجرة تخرج من طور سيناء تنبت بالدهن وصبغ للآكلين
1.english: And [We brought forth] a tree issuing from Mount Sinai which produces oil and food for those who eat.
1.transliteration: Wa shajaratan takhruju min Toori Sainaaa'a tambutu bidduhni wa sibghil lil aakileen

1.arabic: وإن لكم في الأنعام لعبرة ۖ نسقيكم مما في بطونها ولكم فيها منافع كثيرة ومنها تأكلون
1.english: And indeed, for you in livestock is a lesson. We give you drink from that which is in their bellies, and for you in them are numerous benefits, and from them you eat.
1.transliteration: Wa inna lakum fil an'aami la'ibrah; nusqeekum mimmaa fee butoonihaa wa lakum feehaa manaafi'u kaseeratunw wa minhaa taakuloon

1.arabic: وعليها وعلى الفلك تحملون
1.english: And upon them and on ships you are carried.
1.transliteration: Wa 'alaihaa wa'alal fulki tuhmaloon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا نوحا إلى قومه فقال يا قوم اعبدوا الله ما لكم من إله غيره ۖ أفلا تتقون
1.english: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him; then will you not fear Him?"
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa Noohan ilaa qawmihee faqaala yaa qawmi'budul laaha maa lakum min ilahin ghairuhoo afalaa tattaqoon

1.arabic: فقال الملأ الذين كفروا من قومه ما هذا إلا بشر مثلكم يريد أن يتفضل عليكم ولو شاء الله لأنزل ملائكة ما سمعنا بهذا في آبائنا الأولين
1.english: But the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "This is not but a man like yourselves who wishes to take precedence over you; and if Allah had willed [to send a messenger], He would have sent down angels. We have not heard of this among our forefathers.
1.transliteration: Faqaalal mala'ul lazeena kafaroo min qawmihee maa haazaaa illaa basharum mislukum yureedu ai yatafaddala 'alaikum wa law shaaa'al laahu la anzala malaaa'ikatam maa sami'naa bihaazaa feee aabaaa'inal awwaleen

1.arabic: إن هو إلا رجل به جنة فتربصوا به حتى حين
1.english: He is not but a man possessed with madness, so wait concerning him for a time."
1.transliteration: In huwa illaa rajulum bihee jinnatun fatarabbasoo bihee hattan heen

1.arabic: قال رب انصرني بما كذبون
1.english: [Noah] said, "My Lord, support me because they have denied me."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbin surnee bimaa kazzaboon

1.arabic: فأوحينا إليه أن اصنع الفلك بأعيننا ووحينا فإذا جاء أمرنا وفار التنور ۙ فاسلك فيها من كل زوجين اثنين وأهلك إلا من سبق عليه القول منهم ۖ ولا تخاطبني في الذين ظلموا ۖ إنهم مغرقون
1.english: So We inspired to him, "Construct the ship under Our observation, and Our inspiration, and when Our command comes and the oven overflows, put into the ship from each [creature] two mates and your family, except those for whom the decree [of destruction] has proceeded. And do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are to be drowned.
1.transliteration: Fa awhainaaa ilaihi anis na'il fulka bi a'yuninaa wa wahyinaa fa izaa jaaa'a amrunaa wa faarat tannooru fasluk feehaa min kullin zawjainis naini wa ahlaka illaa man sabaqa 'alaihil qawlu minhum wa laa tukhaat ibnee fil lazeena zalamooo innaahum mughraqoon

1.arabic: فإذا استويت أنت ومن معك على الفلك فقل الحمد لله الذي نجانا من القوم الظالمين
1.english: And when you have boarded the ship, you and those with you, then say, 'Praise to Allah who has saved us from the wrongdoing people.'
1.transliteration: Fa izas tawaita ata wa mam ma'aka 'alal fulki faqulil hamdu lillaahil lazee najjaanaa minal qawmiz zalimeen

1.arabic: وقل رب أنزلني منزلا مباركا وأنت خير المنزلين
1.english: And say, 'My Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, and You are the best to accommodate [us].' "
1.transliteration: Wa qur Rabbi anzilnee munzalam mubaarakanw wa Anta khairul munzileen

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآيات وإن كنا لمبتلين
1.english: Indeed in that are signs, and indeed, We are ever testing [Our servants].
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayaatinw wa in kunnaa lamubtaleen

1.arabic: ثم أنشأنا من بعدهم قرنا آخرين
1.english: Then We produced after them a generation of others.
1.transliteration: Summaa anshaana mim ba'dihim qarnan aakhareen

1.arabic: فأرسلنا فيهم رسولا منهم أن اعبدوا الله ما لكم من إله غيره ۖ أفلا تتقون
1.english: And We sent among them a messenger from themselves, [saying], "Worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him; then will you not fear Him?"
1.transliteration: Fa arsalnaa feehim Rasoolam minhum ani'budul laaha maa lakum min ilaahin ghairuhoo afalaa tattaqoon

1.arabic: وقال الملأ من قومه الذين كفروا وكذبوا بلقاء الآخرة وأترفناهم في الحياة الدنيا ما هذا إلا بشر مثلكم يأكل مما تأكلون منه ويشرب مما تشربون
1.english: And the eminent among his people who disbelieved and denied the meeting of the Hereafter while We had given them luxury in the worldly life said, "This is not but a man like yourselves. He eats of that from which you eat and drinks of what you drink.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal mala-u min qawmihil lazeena kafaroo wa kazzaboo bi liqaaa'il Aakhirati wa atrafnaahum fil hayaatid dunyaa maa haazaaa illaa basharum mislukum yaakulu mimmaa taakuloona minhu wa yashrabu mimmaa tashraboon

1.arabic: ولئن أطعتم بشرا مثلكم إنكم إذا لخاسرون
1.english: And if you should obey a man like yourselves, indeed, you would then be losers.
1.transliteration: Wa la'in at'atum basharam mislakum innakum izal lakhaasiroon

1.arabic: أيعدكم أنكم إذا متم وكنتم ترابا وعظاما أنكم مخرجون
1.english: Does he promise you that when you have died and become dust and bones that you will be brought forth [once more]?
1.transliteration: A-Ya'idukum annakum izaa mittum wa kuntum turaabanw wa izaaman annakum mukhrajoon

1.arabic: ۞ هيهات هيهات لما توعدون
1.english: How far, how far, is that which you are promised.
1.transliteration: Haihaata haihaata limaa too'adoon

1.arabic: إن هي إلا حياتنا الدنيا نموت ونحيا وما نحن بمبعوثين
1.english: Life is not but our worldly life - we die and live, but we will not be resurrected.
1.transliteration: In hiya illaa hayaatunad dunyaa namootu wa nahyaa wa maa nahnu bimab'ooseen

1.arabic: إن هو إلا رجل افترى على الله كذبا وما نحن له بمؤمنين
1.english: He is not but a man who has invented a lie about Allah, and we will not believe him."
1.transliteration: In huwa illaa rajulunif taraa 'alal laahi kazibanw wa maa nahnuu lahoo bimu'mineen

1.arabic: قال رب انصرني بما كذبون
1.english: He said, "My Lord, support me because they have denied me."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbin surnee bimaa kazzaboon

1.arabic: قال عما قليل ليصبحن نادمين
1.english: [Allah] said, "After a little, they will surely become regretful."
1.transliteration: Qaala 'ammaa qaleelil la yusbihunna naadimeen

1.arabic: فأخذتهم الصيحة بالحق فجعلناهم غثاء ۚ فبعدا للقوم الظالمين
1.english: So the shriek seized them in truth, and We made them as [plant] stubble. Then away with the wrongdoing people.
1.transliteration: Fa akhazat humus saihatu bilhaqqi faja'alnaahum ghusaaa'aa; fabu;dal lilqaw miz zaalimeen

1.arabic: ثم أنشأنا من بعدهم قرونا آخرين
1.english: Then We produced after them other generations.
1.transliteration: Summa anshaanaa mim ba'dihim quroonan aakhareen

1.arabic: ما تسبق من أمة أجلها وما يستأخرون
1.english: No nation will precede its time [of termination], nor will they remain [thereafter].
1.transliteration: Maa tasbiqu min ummatin ajalahaa wa maa yastaakhiroon

1.arabic: ثم أرسلنا رسلنا تترى ۖ كل ما جاء أمة رسولها كذبوه ۚ فأتبعنا بعضهم بعضا وجعلناهم أحاديث ۚ فبعدا لقوم لا يؤمنون
1.english: Then We sent Our messengers in succession. Every time there came to a nation its messenger, they denied him, so We made them follow one another [to destruction], and We made them narrations. So away with a people who do not believe.
1.transliteration: Summa arsalnaa Rusulanaa tatraa kulla maa jaaa'a ummatar Rasooluhaa kazzabooh; fa atba'naa ba'dahum ba'danw wa ja'alnaahum ahaadees; fabu'dal liqawmil laa yu'minoon

1.arabic: ثم أرسلنا موسى وأخاه هارون بآياتنا وسلطان مبين
1.english: Then We sent Moses and his brother Aaron with Our signs and a clear authority
1.transliteration: Summa arsalnaa Moosaa wa akhaahu Haaroona bi Aayaatinaa wa sultaanim mubeen

1.arabic: إلى فرعون وملئه فاستكبروا وكانوا قوما عالين
1.english: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they were arrogant and were a haughty people.
1.transliteration: Ilaa Fir'awna wa mala'ihee fastakbaroo wa kaanoo qawman 'aaleem

1.arabic: فقالوا أنؤمن لبشرين مثلنا وقومهما لنا عابدون
1.english: They said, "Should we believe two men like ourselves while their people are for us in servitude?"
1.transliteration: Faqaaloo annu'minu libasharaini mislinaa wa qawmuhumaa lanaa 'aabidoon

1.arabic: فكذبوهما فكانوا من المهلكين
1.english: So they denied them and were of those destroyed.
1.transliteration: Fakazzaboohumaa fakaanoo minal mmuhlakeen

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى الكتاب لعلهم يهتدون
1.english: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture that perhaps they would be guided.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Moosal Kitaaba la'allahum yahtadoon

1.arabic: وجعلنا ابن مريم وأمه آية وآويناهما إلى ربوة ذات قرار ومعين
1.english: And We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign and sheltered them within a high ground having level [areas] and flowing water.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnab na Maryama wa ummahooo aayatannw wa aawainaahumaaa ilaa rabwatin zaati qaraarinw wa ma'een

1.arabic: يا أيها الرسل كلوا من الطيبات واعملوا صالحا ۖ إني بما تعملون عليم
1.english: [Allah said], "O messengers, eat from the good foods and work righteousness. Indeed, I, of what you do, am Knowing.
1.transliteration: Yaaa aiyuhar Rusulu kuloo minat taiyibaati wa'maloo saalihan innee bimaa ta'maloona 'Aleem

1.arabic: وإن هذه أمتكم أمة واحدة وأنا ربكم فاتقون
1.english: And indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so fear Me."
1.transliteration: Wa inna haaziheee ummatukum ummatanw waahidatanw wa Ana Rabbukum fattaqoon

1.arabic: فتقطعوا أمرهم بينهم زبرا ۖ كل حزب بما لديهم فرحون
1.english: But the people divided their religion among them into sects - each faction, in what it has, rejoicing.
1.transliteration: Fataqatta'ooo amrahum bainahum zuburaa; kullu hizbim bimaa ladaihim farihoon

1.arabic: فذرهم في غمرتهم حتى حين
1.english: So leave them in their confusion for a time.
1.transliteration: Fazarhum fee ghamratihim hattaa heen

1.arabic: أيحسبون أنما نمدهم به من مال وبنين
1.english: Do they think that what We extend to them of wealth and children
1.transliteration: A-yahsaboona annnamaa numiduhum bihee mimmaalinw wa baneen

1.arabic: نسارع لهم في الخيرات ۚ بل لا يشعرون
1.english: Is [because] We hasten for them good things? Rather, they do not perceive.
1.transliteration: Nusaari'u lahum fil khairaat; bal laa yash'uroon

1.arabic: إن الذين هم من خشية ربهم مشفقون
1.english: Indeed, they who are apprehensive from fear of their Lord
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena hum min khashyati Rabbihim mushfiqoon

1.arabic: والذين هم بآيات ربهم يؤمنون
1.english: And they who believe in the signs of their Lord
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum bi Aayaati Rabbihim yu'minoon

1.arabic: والذين هم بربهم لا يشركون
1.english: And they who do not associate anything with their Lord
1.transliteration: Wallazeena hum bi Rabbihim laa yushrikoon

1.arabic: والذين يؤتون ما آتوا وقلوبهم وجلة أنهم إلى ربهم راجعون
1.english: And they who give what they give while their hearts are fearful because they will be returning to their Lord -
1.transliteration: Wallazeena yu'toona maaa aataw wa quloobuhum wajilatun annahum ilaa Rabbihim raaji'oon

1.arabic: أولئك يسارعون في الخيرات وهم لها سابقون
1.english: It is those who hasten to good deeds, and they outstrip [others] therein.
1.transliteration: Ulaaa'ika yusaari'oona fil khairaati wa hum lahaa saabiqoon

1.arabic: ولا نكلف نفسا إلا وسعها ۖ ولدينا كتاب ينطق بالحق ۚ وهم لا يظلمون
1.english: And We charge no soul except [with that within] its capacity, and with Us is a record which speaks with truth; and they will not be wronged.
1.transliteration: Wa laa nukallifu nafsan illaa wus'ahaa wa ladainaa kitaabuny yantiqu bilhaqqi w ahum la yuzlamoon

1.arabic: بل قلوبهم في غمرة من هذا ولهم أعمال من دون ذلك هم لها عاملون
1.english: But their hearts are covered with confusion over this, and they have [evil] deeds besides disbelief which they are doing,
1.transliteration: Bal quloobuhum fee ghamratim min haazaa wa lahum a'maalum min dooni zaalika hum lahaa 'aamiloon

1.arabic: حتى إذا أخذنا مترفيهم بالعذاب إذا هم يجأرون
1.english: Until when We seize their affluent ones with punishment, at once they are crying [to Allah] for help.
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaaa akhznaa mutrafeehim bil'azaabi izaa hum yaj'aroon

1.arabic: لا تجأروا اليوم ۖ إنكم منا لا تنصرون
1.english: Do not cry out today. Indeed, by Us you will not be helped.
1.transliteration: Laa taj'arul yawma innakum minnaa laa tunsaroon

1.arabic: قد كانت آياتي تتلى عليكم فكنتم على أعقابكم تنكصون
1.english: My verses had already been recited to you, but you were turning back on your heels
1.transliteration: Qad kaanat Aayaatee tutlaa 'alaikum fakuntum 'alaaa a'qaabikum tankisoon

1.arabic: مستكبرين به سامرا تهجرون
1.english: In arrogance regarding it, conversing by night, speaking evil.
1.transliteration: Mustakbireena bihee saamiran tahjuroon

1.arabic: أفلم يدبروا القول أم جاءهم ما لم يأت آباءهم الأولين
1.english: Then have they not reflected over the Qur'an, or has there come to them that which had not come to their forefathers?
1.transliteration: Afalam yaddabbarrul qawla am jaaa'ahum maa lam yaati aabaaa'ahumul awwaleen

1.arabic: أم لم يعرفوا رسولهم فهم له منكرون
1.english: Or did they not know their Messenger, so they are toward him disacknowledging?
1.transliteration: Am lam ya'rifoo Rasoolahum fahum lahoo munkiroon

1.arabic: أم يقولون به جنة ۚ بل جاءهم بالحق وأكثرهم للحق كارهون
1.english: Or do they say, "In him is madness?" Rather, he brought them the truth, but most of them, to the truth, are averse.
1.transliteration: Am yaqooloona bihee jinnnah; bal jaaa'ahum bilhaqqi wa aksaruhum lil haqqi kaarihoon

1.arabic: ولو اتبع الحق أهواءهم لفسدت السماوات والأرض ومن فيهن ۚ بل أتيناهم بذكرهم فهم عن ذكرهم معرضون
1.english: But if the Truth had followed their inclinations, the heavens and the earth and whoever is in them would have been ruined. Rather, We have brought them their message, but they, from their message, are turning away.
1.transliteration: Wa lawit taba'al haqqu ahwaaa'ahum lafasadatis samaawaatu wal ardu wa man feehinnn; bal atainaahum bizikrihim fahum 'an zikrihim mu'ridon

1.arabic: أم تسألهم خرجا فخراج ربك خير ۖ وهو خير الرازقين
1.english: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask them for payment? But the reward of your Lord is best, and He is the best of providers.
1.transliteration: Am tas'aluhum kharjan fakharaaju Rabbika khairunw wa Huwa khairur raaziqeen

1.arabic: وإنك لتدعوهم إلى صراط مستقيم
1.english: And indeed, you invite them to a straight path.
1.transliteration: Wa innaka latad'oohum ilaa Siraatim Mustaqeem

1.arabic: وإن الذين لا يؤمنون بالآخرة عن الصراط لناكبون
1.english: But indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are deviating from the path.
1.transliteration: Wa innnal lazeena laa yu'minoona bil Aakhirati 'anis siraati lanaakiboon

1.arabic: ۞ ولو رحمناهم وكشفنا ما بهم من ضر للجوا في طغيانهم يعمهون
1.english: And even if We gave them mercy and removed what was upon them of affliction, they would persist in their transgression, wandering blindly.
1.transliteration: Wa law rahimnaahum wa kashafnaa maa bihim min durril lalajjoo fee tughyaanihim ya'mahoon

1.arabic: ولقد أخذناهم بالعذاب فما استكانوا لربهم وما يتضرعون
1.english: And We had gripped them with suffering [as a warning], but they did not yield to their Lord, nor did they humbly supplicate, [and will continue thus]
1.transliteration: Wa laqad akhaznaahum bil'azaabi famastakaanoo li Rabbihim wa maa yatadarra'oon

1.arabic: حتى إذا فتحنا عليهم بابا ذا عذاب شديد إذا هم فيه مبلسون
1.english: Until when We have opened before them a door of severe punishment, immediately they will be therein in despair.
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa fatahnaa 'alaihim baaban zaa 'azaabin shadeedin izaa hum feehi mublisoon

1.arabic: وهو الذي أنشأ لكم السمع والأبصار والأفئدة ۚ قليلا ما تشكرون
1.english: And it is He who produced for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazeee ansha a-lakumus sam'a wal absaara wal af'idah; qaleelam maa tashkuroon

1.arabic: وهو الذي ذرأكم في الأرض وإليه تحشرون
1.english: And it is He who has multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee zara akum fil ardi wa ilaihi tuhsharoon

1.arabic: وهو الذي يحيي ويميت وله اختلاف الليل والنهار ۚ أفلا تعقلون
1.english: And it is He who gives life and causes death, and His is the alternation of the night and the day. Then will you not reason?
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee yuhyee wa yumeetu wa lahukh tilaaful laili wannahaar; afalaa ta'qiloon

1.arabic: بل قالوا مثل ما قال الأولون
1.english: Rather, they say like what the former peoples said.
1.transliteration: Bal qaaloo misla maa qaalal awwaloon

1.arabic: قالوا أإذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظاما أإنا لمبعوثون
1.english: They said, "When we have died and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?
1.transliteration: Qaalooo 'a-izaa mitnaa wa kunnaa turaabanw wa 'izaaman 'a-innaa lamab 'oosoon

1.arabic: لقد وعدنا نحن وآباؤنا هذا من قبل إن هذا إلا أساطير الأولين
1.english: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before; this is not but legends of the former peoples."
1.transliteration: Laqad wu'idnaa nahnu wa aabaaa'unaa haazaa min qablu in haazaaa illaaa asaateerul awwaleen

1.arabic: قل لمن الأرض ومن فيها إن كنتم تعلمون
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "To whom belongs the earth and whoever is in it, if you should know?"
1.transliteration: Qul limanil ardu wa man feehaaa in kuntum ta'lamoon

1.arabic: سيقولون لله ۚ قل أفلا تذكرون
1.english: They will say, "To Allah." Say, "Then will you not remember?"
1.transliteration: Sa-yaqooloona lillaah; qul afalaa tazakkkaroon

1.arabic: قل من رب السماوات السبع ورب العرش العظيم
1.english: Say, "Who is Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Great Throne?"
1.transliteration: Qul mar Rabbus samaawaatis sab'i wa Rabbul 'Arshil 'Azeem

1.arabic: سيقولون لله ۚ قل أفلا تتقون
1.english: They will say, "[They belong] to Allah." Say, "Then will you not fear Him?"
1.transliteration: Sa yaqooloona lillaah; qul afalaa tattaqoon

1.arabic: قل من بيده ملكوت كل شيء وهو يجير ولا يجار عليه إن كنتم تعلمون
1.english: Say, "In whose hand is the realm of all things - and He protects while none can protect against Him - if you should know?"
1.transliteration: Qul mam bi yadihee malakootu kulli shai'inw wa Huwa yujeeru wa laa yujaaru 'alaihi in kuntum ta'lamoon

1.arabic: سيقولون لله ۚ قل فأنى تسحرون
1.english: They will say, "[All belongs] to Allah." Say, "Then how are you deluded?"
1.transliteration: Sa yaqooloona lillaah; qul fa annaa tus haroon

1.arabic: بل أتيناهم بالحق وإنهم لكاذبون
1.english: Rather, We have brought them the truth, and indeed they are liars.
1.transliteration: Bal atainaahum bil haqqi wa innahum lakaaziboon

1.arabic: ما اتخذ الله من ولد وما كان معه من إله ۚ إذا لذهب كل إله بما خلق ولعلا بعضهم على بعض ۚ سبحان الله عما يصفون
1.english: Allah has not taken any son, nor has there ever been with Him any deity. [If there had been], then each deity would have taken what it created, and some of them would have sought to overcome others. Exalted is Allah above what they describe [concerning Him].
1.transliteration: Mat takhazal laahu minw waladinw wa maa kaana ma'ahoo min ilaah; izal lazahaba kullu ilaahim bimaa khalaqa wa la'alaa ba'duhum 'alaa ba'd; Subhaannal laahi 'ammaa yasifoon

1.arabic: عالم الغيب والشهادة فتعالى عما يشركون
1.english: [He is] Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, so high is He above what they associate [with Him].
1.transliteration: 'Aalimil Ghaibi wash shahhaadati fata'aalaa 'ammaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: قل رب إما تريني ما يوعدون
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "My Lord, if You should show me that which they are promised,
1.transliteration: Qur Rabbi immmaa turiyannee maa yoo'adoon

1.arabic: رب فلا تجعلني في القوم الظالمين
1.english: My Lord, then do not place me among the wrongdoing people."
1.transliteration: Rabbi falaa taj'alnee fil qawmiz zaalimeen

1.arabic: وإنا على أن نريك ما نعدهم لقادرون
1.english: And indeed, We are able to show you what We have promised them.
1.transliteration: Wa innaa 'alaaa an nuriyaka maa na'iduhum laqaadiroon

1.arabic: ادفع بالتي هي أحسن السيئة ۚ نحن أعلم بما يصفون
1.english: Repel, by [means of] what is best, [their] evil. We are most knowing of what they describe.
1.transliteration: Idfa' billate hiya ahsanus saiyi'ah; nahnu a'lamu bimaa yasifoon

1.arabic: وقل رب أعوذ بك من همزات الشياطين
1.english: And say, "My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the incitements of the devils,
1.transliteration: Wa qur Rabbi a'oozu bika min hamazaatish Shayaateen

1.arabic: وأعوذ بك رب أن يحضرون
1.english: And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they be present with me."
1.transliteration: Wa a'oozu bika Rabbi ai-yahduroon

1.arabic: حتى إذا جاء أحدهم الموت قال رب ارجعون
1.english: [For such is the state of the disbelievers], until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "My Lord, send me back
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa jaaa'a ahada humul mawtu qaala Rabbir ji'oon

1.arabic: لعلي أعمل صالحا فيما تركت ۚ كلا ۚ إنها كلمة هو قائلها ۖ ومن ورائهم برزخ إلى يوم يبعثون
1.english: That I might do righteousness in that which I left behind." No! It is only a word he is saying; and behind them is a barrier until the Day they are resurrected.
1.transliteration: La'alleee a'malu saalihan feemaa taraktu kallaa; innahaa kalimatun huwa qaaa'iluhaa wa minw waraaa'him barzakhun ilaa Yawmi yub'asoon

1.arabic: فإذا نفخ في الصور فلا أنساب بينهم يومئذ ولا يتساءلون
1.english: So when the Horn is blown, no relationship will there be among them that Day, nor will they ask about one another.
1.transliteration: Fa izaa nufikha fis Soori falaaa ansaaba bainahum yawma'izinw wa laa yatasaaa'aloon

1.arabic: فمن ثقلت موازينه فأولئك هم المفلحون
1.english: And those whose scales are heavy [with good deeds] - it is they who are the successful.
1.transliteration: Faman saqulat mawaazee nuhoo fa ulaaa'ika humul muflihoon

1.arabic: ومن خفت موازينه فأولئك الذين خسروا أنفسهم في جهنم خالدون
1.english: But those whose scales are light - those are the ones who have lost their souls, [being] in Hell, abiding eternally.
1.transliteration: Wa man khaffat mawaa zeenuhoo fa ulaaa'ikal lazeena khasiroon anfusahum fee Jahannnama khaalidoon

1.arabic: تلفح وجوههم النار وهم فيها كالحون
1.english: The Fire will sear their faces, and they therein will have taut smiles.
1.transliteration: Talfahu wujoohahumun Naaru wa hum feehaa kaalihood

1.arabic: ألم تكن آياتي تتلى عليكم فكنتم بها تكذبون
1.english: [It will be said]. "Were not My verses recited to you and you used to deny them?"
1.transliteration: Alam takun Aayaatee tutlaa 'alaikum fakuntum bihaa tukazziboon

1.arabic: قالوا ربنا غلبت علينا شقوتنا وكنا قوما ضالين
1.english: They will say, "Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a people astray.
1.transliteration: Qaaloo Rabbanaa ghalabat 'alainaa shiqwatunaa wa kunnaa qawman daaalleen

1.arabic: ربنا أخرجنا منها فإن عدنا فإنا ظالمون
1.english: Our Lord, remove us from it, and if we were to return [to evil], we would indeed be wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Rabbanaa akhrijnaa minhaa fa in 'udnaa fa innaa zaalimoon

1.arabic: قال اخسئوا فيها ولا تكلمون
1.english: He will say, "Remain despised therein and do not speak to Me.
1.transliteration: Qaalakh sa'oo feehaa wa laa tukallimoon

1.arabic: إنه كان فريق من عبادي يقولون ربنا آمنا فاغفر لنا وارحمنا وأنت خير الراحمين
1.english: Indeed, there was a party of My servants who said, 'Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of the merciful.'
1.transliteration: Innahoo kaana fareequm min 'ibaadee yaqooloona Rabbanaaa aamannaa faghfir lanaa warhamnaa wa Anta khairur raahimeen

1.arabic: فاتخذتموهم سخريا حتى أنسوكم ذكري وكنتم منهم تضحكون
1.english: But you took them in mockery to the point that they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.
1.transliteration: Fattakhaztumoohum sikhriyyan hattaaa ansawkum zikree wa kuntum minhum tadhakoon

1.arabic: إني جزيتهم اليوم بما صبروا أنهم هم الفائزون
1.english: Indeed, I have rewarded them this Day for their patient endurance - that they are the attainers [of success]."
1.transliteration: Inee jazaituhumul Yawma bimaa sabarooo annahum humul faaa'izoon

1.arabic: قال كم لبثتم في الأرض عدد سنين
1.english: [Allah] will say, "How long did you remain on earth in number of years?"
1.transliteration: Qaala kam labistum fil ardi 'adada sineen

1.arabic: قالوا لبثنا يوما أو بعض يوم فاسأل العادين
1.english: They will say, "We remained a day or part of a day; ask those who enumerate."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo labisnaa yawman aw ba'da yawmin fas'alil 'aaaddeen

1.arabic: قال إن لبثتم إلا قليلا ۖ لو أنكم كنتم تعلمون
1.english: He will say, "You stayed not but a little - if only you had known.
1.transliteration: Qaala il labistum illaa qaleelal law annakum kuntum ta'lamoon

1.arabic: أفحسبتم أنما خلقناكم عبثا وأنكم إلينا لا ترجعون
1.english: Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?"
1.transliteration: Afahsibtum annamaa khalaqnaakum 'abasanw wa annakum ilainaa laa turja'oon

1.arabic: فتعالى الله الملك الحق ۖ لا إله إلا هو رب العرش الكريم
1.english: So exalted is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth; there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.
1.transliteration: Fata'aalal laahul Malikul Haqq; laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa Rabbul 'Arshil Kareem

1.arabic: ومن يدع مع الله إلها آخر لا برهان له به فإنما حسابه عند ربه ۚ إنه لا يفلح الكافرون
1.english: And whoever invokes besides Allah another deity for which he has no proof - then his account is only with his Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will not succeed.
1.transliteration: Wa mai yad'u ma'allaahi ilaahan aakhara laa burhaana lahoo bihee fa innnamaa hisaabuhoo 'inda Rabbih; innahoo laa yuflihul kaafiroon

1.arabic: وقل رب اغفر وارحم وأنت خير الراحمين
1.english: And, [O Muhammad], say, "My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful."
1.transliteration: Wa qur Rabbigh fir warham wa Anta khairur raahimeen
